title: Upload Admission, Untrusted Files, and Quarantine · Mastery Check
summary: A production review compares near-miss decisions for upload admission, untrusted files, and quarantine and accepts only bounded behavior, causal changed-case evidence, and an explicit AWS transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for bounded upload-admission and quarantine workflow. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Enforce per-file, request, tenant, concurrent, time, and aggregate byte budgets before and during transfer.
Treat filenames as display metadata, normalize safely, reject controls and path confusion, and generate storage keys independently.
Combine bounded signature inspection, declared media type, extension, parser or scanner evidence, and allow-list policy without executing content.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Upload Accessible Feedback
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go file-delivery team handles case 7978 during a high-traffic evidence intake window. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound upload bytes and concurrency before quarantined publication state; record cleanup, rollback, restoration, and residual-risk ownership; then cancel halfway through and prove every body, part, and goroutine reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Expose preflight limits, progress, cancellation, validation details, recovery, and non-color status to keyboard and assistive-technology users.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Upload Accessible Feedback.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for expose preflight limits, progress, cancellation, validation details, recovery, and non-color status to keyboard and assistive-technology users. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Upload Accessible Feedback, keep Upload Accessible Feedback connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Upload Accessible Feedback
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Upload Accessible Feedback: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Go file-delivery team handles case 2407 during a compliance restore drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound upload bytes and concurrency before quarantined publication state; preserve accessible progress, cancellation, status, and recovery evidence; then change region, range, or signed header and explain the exact failure boundary. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Expose preflight limits, progress, cancellation, validation details, recovery, and non-color status to keyboard and assistive-technology users.

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Upload Accessible Feedback.
steps.why: Retrieving Upload Accessible Feedback through the concrete bounded upload-admission and quarantine workflow case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.
